title: Trump says Iran peace deal could be reached ‘soon’ after Tehran reopens Hormuz Strait
description: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon". But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences.

title: Day 5 of Pope's African tour
description: Day 5 in Africa for Pope Leo the 14th. In the midst of a pause in the ongoing violence in Cameroon, the Pope headed to Douala after the city of Bamenda, to celebrate a Mass. Also It's now more than 48 hours since Million Beyene disappeared without a trace. And Literature takes centre stage in the French capital as Paris hosts the fifth edition of its Book Festival, among the standout voices, Cameroonian writer Djaïli Amadou Amal.

title: Macron, Meloni, Starmer and Merz welcome Hormuz Strait reopening
description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announcement that the waterway would be reopened to commercial shipping for the rest of the ceasefire, but called for the strait's "unconditional" reopening.

title: 1.2 million people uprooted by war
description: More than a million civilians in Lebanon have been displaced since the war in the Middle East erupted at the end of February. Among them are thousands of young mothers and pregnant women, many forced to give birth in makeshift camps or other inadequate conditions. This report from our colleagues at France 2, by Camille Nedelec, takes a closer look.

title: EU moves to tighten rules on harmful pollutants: The invisible danger of 'forever chemicals'
description: They are known as "forever chemicals": synthetic compounds that resist breakdown in the human body and the environment. Also known as PFAS, there are thousands of them, and you might not even realise that you are being exposed to them while doing simple everyday tasks like cooking. title: Where is US-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin?
description: This week, FRANCE 24's media show Scoop looks at growing international alarm after US-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin was detained in Kuwait. Prior to his arrest, Shihab-Eldin shared publicly available videos and images related to the Iran war. Press and human rights groups say that his detention comes amid increasingly tight censorship in Kuwait and in the broader Gulf. Our guest is Sara Qudah from the Committee to Protect Journalists.

title: A cultural stroll through the French Caribbean island of Martinique
description: This week we're taking our cameras over 6,000 km from Paris, to the French Caribbean island of Martinique. Visual artist Valérie John opens the doors of her "studio-artwork" in La Trinité, where indigo lies at the heart of everything she does. Far more than just a colour, it's a way of connecting with memory, identity and cultural heritage. "My work is a factory for producing creolisation," she tells us.

title: Live: Trump welcomes Iran's announcement that Strait of Hormuz is fully open to shipping

France24 International Affairs editor Rochelle Ferguson shares further insights.

title: Britain, France chair meeting on Hormuz
description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. title: Living under threat of landslides: The Italian villages at risk of disappearing

The post Italys opposition leader, whose father is Jewish, backs PM in split with Trump over Israel and pope appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Trump official defends seeking identities of Jews at Penn for campus antisemitism probe
description:
Head of US Equal Employment Opportunity Commission insists 'there is no other way to protect victims... unless you collect info about them,' as others warn against 'compiling lists of Jews' 
The post Trump official defends seeking identities of Jews at Penn for campus antisemitism probe appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Ceasefire appears to hold overnight as Trump tells Hezbollah to ‘act nicely and well’
description:
Lebanon's army accuses IDF of shelling the country in violation of 10-day truce * Senior Iranian leader claims credit for ceasefire as residents who fled start returning to Hezbollah’s Beirut-area stronghold
The post Ceasefire appears to hold overnight as Trump tells Hezbollah to ‘act nicely and well’ appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: ‘Safe haven’: Iranian soccer players who sought asylum in Australia thank supporters
description:
Fatemeh Pasandideh and Atefeh Ramezanisadeh, the only two members of the team who ultimately remained behind, say they hope to continue their sporting careers in new home
The post Safe haven: Iranian soccer players who sought asylum in Australia thank supporters appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: A Tale of Two Sisters
description:
Grace and Carol, straddling the ninth decade of their lives, suddenly find themselves as unexpected roommates during a war.
The post A Tale of Two Sisters appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Germany, Ukraine sign defence deal on missiles, drones, and data
description: Germany and Ukraine will cooperate on missile deliveries, drone technology, and battlefield data exchange.

title: Australian ex-soldier Roberts-Smith granted bail in Afghan war crimes case
description: Former soldier is accused of murdering five people in Afghanistan.

title: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth reads fake Bible verse from Pulp Fiction
description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth read a prayer drawn from a misquote of Ezekiel 25:17 in Pulp Fiction.
